notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.
notes.contentHtml:
THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.


For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors, visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: The Microbiology B.S. is designed for students interested in careers in biomedical, environmental, and basic science fields in both industry and academia. As a unique feature of this program, students take a microbiology course with a focus on evaluating relevant scientific articles. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer students must complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: General Chemistry series CHEM 8A – Organic Chemistry BIOL 20A – Cell and Molecular Biology BIOL 20L – Experimental Biology Laboratory BIOL 20L is not required for students who have completed BIOL 20A and BIOE 20B articulated equivalents at California Community Colleges. BIOE 20B – Development and Physiology One math course: MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications MATH 16A – Mathematics for Life and Environmental Sciences MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. It is strongly recommended that community college students complete all required General Chemistry coursework for their intended major at one institution prior to transfer to UC Santa Cruz. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below, especially the following: CHEM 8B – Organic Chemistry CHEM 8L – Organic Chemistry Laboratory MATH 11B – Calculus with Applications OR MATH 16B – Mathematics for Life and Environmental Sciences OR MATH 19B – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics STAT 5 – Statistics OR STAT 7 – Statistical Methods for the Biological, Environmental, and Health Sciences & STAT 7L – Statistical Methods for the Biological, Environmental, and Health Sciences Laboratory These courses are prerequisites for upper-division courses required for this major. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Additional Information for Transfer Prospective transfer students should prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses, and may additionally complete courses that satisfy UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as time allows. For information on incorporating general education requirements into your pre-transfer academic plan, consult with your community college counselor. Visit the Microbiology and Environmental Toxicology website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Microbiology B.S. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
The Microbiology B.S. is designed for students interested in careers in biomedical, environmental, and basic science fields in both industry and academia. As a unique feature of this program, students take a microbiology course with a focus on evaluating relevant scientific articles.


Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer students must complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall:


  • General Chemistry series
  • CHEM 8A – Organic Chemistry
  • BIOL 20A – Cell and Molecular Biology
  • BIOL 20L – Experimental Biology Laboratory
  • BIOL 20L is not required for students who have completed BIOL 20A and BIOE 20B articulated equivalents at California Community Colleges.
  • BIOE 20B – Development and Physiology
  • One math course:
  • MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications
  • MATH 16A – Mathematics for Life and Environmental Sciences
  • MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics


Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements.


It is strongly recommended that community college students complete all required General Chemistry coursework for their intended major at one institution prior to transfer to UC Santa Cruz.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below, especially the following:


  • CHEM 8B – Organic Chemistry
  • CHEM 8L – Organic Chemistry Laboratory
  • MATH 11B – Calculus with Applications OR MATH 16B – Mathematics for Life and Environmental Sciences OR MATH 19B – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
  • STAT 5 – Statistics OR STAT 7 – Statistical Methods for the Biological, Environmental, and Health Sciences & STAT 7L – Statistical Methods for the Biological, Environmental, and Health Sciences Laboratory


These courses are prerequisites for upper-division courses required for this major. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree.



Additional Information for Transfer

Prospective transfer students should prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses, and may additionally complete courses that satisfy UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as time allows. For information on incorporating general education requirements into your pre-transfer academic plan, consult with your community college counselor.


Visit the Microbiology and Environmental Toxicology website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Microbiology B.S. Program Statement
